candidates.why: Direct top-line revenue line; preferred over duplicate total because rules prefer the primary reported line over redundant total rows when values are identical.
Also represents top-line revenue, but appears to duplicate Revenue exactly and is less preferred than the simpler primary label.
Not revenue; this is a cost line and does not match REVT.

variables.notes: Use Revenue first; Total Revenue is a valid fallback if labeling changes by year.

candidates.why: Direct cost-of-sales style line immediately below revenue/gross profit; best match for COGS.
Could be a COGS component, but appears empty and is not the main direct cost line.
Inventory asset, not an income statement expense.

variables.notes: Production costs is the direct production/service cost line and best maps to COGS.

candidates.why: Clear SG&A overhead component; appropriate to include.
Would be an SG&A component if populated, but appears supplemental/empty in preview.
Explicit SG&A label exists, but appears empty in preview; per rules, when explicit SG&A exists but is mostly empty, include it and also closest overhead bucket(s).
Contains depreciation, which must be excluded from XSGA components.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Brands, Patents, Trademarks, Marketing & Artistic Intangibles - Supplemental
candidates.why: Amortization must be excluded from XSGA components.

variables.notes: Used one row outside the candidate list: Distribution costs. Candidate list missed a clear SG&A component present in the statement. Selling, General & Admin is the explicit SG&A row but appears mostly empty in the preview, so per rule it is included along with overhead component rows to improve year coverage. Administrative expenses and Distribution costs are both SG&A-type components. Excluded Reported Operating Expense because it appears to be a broader subtotal and includes non-overhead items. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Distribution costs
No dedicated research and development expense line is visible on the provided Income Statement.

candidates.why: Broad finance-cost line that includes more than pure interest; usable only as fallback.
Direct interest expense line; best match for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest-related, but only a component of total interest expense.
Semantically correct label, but appears empty in preview; still could serve as fallback because presence implies at least one year may exist.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental version of lease interest; lower priority than the main reported line.

variables.notes: Priority list ordered by specificity: direct interest expense first, then alternate label, then broad financial expense as fallback if needed.

candidates.why: Equity component only, not total book equity.
Generic equity label, but appears empty and is less precise than owner-attributable equity.
Best match because it represents parent/shareholder equity and excludes non-controlling interests, avoiding double counting with MIB.
Includes non-controlling interests, so less preferred when a parent/shareholder equity line exists.
Balancing item, not an accounting line to use.
Balance sheet total, not equity.

variables.notes: Preferred owner-attributable equity line to exclude non-controlling interests from BE.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation line, not preferred for balance sheet minority interest.
Income statement allocation line, not preferred for balance sheet minority interest.
Income statement presentation, but balance sheet source is preferred for MIB.
Direct balance sheet NCI line; best match for MIB.
Duplicate-style alternative label for the same concept on the balance sheet; usable fallback.

variables.notes: Priority list uses the balance sheet NCI label first, with the synonymous balance sheet fallback second.
